mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the tightening cycle continues.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49% after multi-year tightening from 1% in 2004
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05) during continued FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window from a cyclical low, it signals labor market deterioration that leads equity markets to price in weaker earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% near cyclical lows as of Feb 2006
failure_condition: In periods when the UNRATE trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional returns (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST of more than 10% from its trailing 6-month peak signals a turn in the housing cycle that precedes broader consumer demand weakness and corporate earnings deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2276k units, near historic highs of the housing boom as of early 2006
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following a HOUST peak-decline trigger are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing CPIAUCSL-based inflation trend (i.e., real short rates turn meaningfully positive), the combined tightening of nominal policy and disinflation raises real borrowing costs beyond what either series alone implies, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is not decomposable into FEDFUNDS or CPIAUCSL trends individually.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPIAUCSL inflation rate > 2pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 4.49% vs CPIAUCSL 199.0 index level implying moderate real rate
regime_hint: positive real rate regime late in tightening cycle
failure_condition: Interaction term (real rate proxy) shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone (delta rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, which positively influences near-term equity sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.269, trending upward as of Jan 2006
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward 5d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or sign hit-rate is not above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, continued strong PAYEMS growth (above trend) signals an overheating labor market that increases the probability of further Fed hikes, which outweighs the positive earnings signal and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In an easing or neutral monetary regime, the same PAYEMS strength would instead be growth-supportive and positive for equities.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5% while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 134,789k with FEDFUNDS at 4.49% and rising as of Feb 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with active monetary tightening
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns under strong PAYEMS growth in tightening regimes are not significantly different from returns under strong PAYEMS growth in easing regimes (interaction coefficient p>0.10), invalidating regime-conditionality.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining HOUST alongside rising FEDFUNDS indicates that higher financing costs are actively suppressing housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer spending contraction; this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter more strongly than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is at or near cycle highs
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST 2276k and FEDFUNDS 4.49% both near multi-year extremes as of early 2006
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with peaking housing activity
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS signal underperforms the single-series HOUST decline signal (H_003) in out-of-sample rank IC, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects robust consumer spending momentum, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 362,294 million dollars, trending upward as of Jan 2006
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or hit-rate does not exceed 52%.
